title: Estimate
description: Effort estimate for a project, with the hierarchy epics -> tasks -> dev_tasks. Everything is measured in working days. This is the single source of truth: the xlsx exports (estimate.xlsx, dev_tasks.xlsx) are generated by the backend from this JSON, there are no separate files for the dev tasks. Totals are NOT stored here — consumers (backend/frontend) compute them by summing epics[].tasks[] + epics[].e2e + contingency_pct. Summing rule: if task.dev_tasks is non-empty, task.days MUST equal the sum of dev_tasks[].days (checked semantically in server/documents.py::save_doc, beyond the schema); if dev_tasks is empty, task.days is the directly estimated value. It also carries assumptions, general considerations and open questions for the users (sheets 'Assumptions and Considerations' and 'Questions for the Users' in the Excel export) — not really optional: any serious estimate has at least one of each.

client.description: Client name. Null if not specified.

date.description: Date of the estimate, YYYY-MM-DD format.

contingency_pct.description: Contingency percentage to apply to the total days (e.g. 15 for 15%).

notes.description: Free-form notes on the estimate (general assumptions, confidence level, and so on).

status.description: Editorial state of the document, for the 'chat stays conversational' UX: 'draft' while it is still being refined with the user in chat (citing elements by id), 'confirmed' once the user has validated the content. Optional field, for compatibility with pre-existing documents that do not carry it yet — a document without 'status' is treated as a draft.

dev_tasks_status.description: Editorial state of the dev_tasks breakdown (separate from 'status', which only covers the estimate itself): 'draft' while the breakdown is being refined in chat, 'confirmed' once the user has validated it. Absent = the breakdown has not been generated for any task yet.

epics.description: The epics of the estimate, in presentation order.

name.description: Epic name, e.g. '1. Epic Name'. It must be identical across every reference (dev_tasks) so that consumers can group rows consistently.

tasks.description: Development tasks of the epic (excluding the E2E row, handled separately in 'e2e').

task.description: Task name, written in the language a project manager would use, not in platform jargon.

days.description: Working days (development + unit test). Minimum 0.25, rounded to the nearest 0.5 above 1 day. If 'dev_tasks' is non-empty this value MUST equal the sum of dev_tasks[].days (checked semantically in server/documents.py::save_doc, beyond the schema) — otherwise it is the directly estimated value.

description.description: Functional description of the task (1-2 sentences, in plain terms: what it does, not how it is built). An empty string is allowed but discouraged — any serious task has a description.

dev_tasks.description: Granular breakdown of the task into sub-tasks that can be assigned to developers (generated by /dev-tasks once the estimate is confirmed). Empty array if the breakdown has not been done for this task yet — in that case 'days' is the directly estimated value.

id.description: Dev task identifier, scoped to the parent task, e.g. 'E2.T3.D1'.

dev_task.description: Name of the granular sub-task: what the developer concretely builds.

description.description: Technical description for the developer (3-10 lines): tables and columns, objects to create, references to the brief, sub-flows, connections, and so on.

days.description: Working days for this dev task.

owner.description: Assigned developer. Empty string if not assigned yet (filled in during planning).

layer.description: Technical layer: it determines the execution order inside the epic on the project timeline. 1 = data model and data structures; 2 = forms and screens; 3 = business rules, processes, integrations, notifications (parallelizable among themselves); 4 = E2E tests. Layers 1-2-3-4 are sequential within the same epic; different epics proceed in parallel.

depends_on.description: Explicit dependency override, for the cases the layer does not capture. Ids of dev tasks that must be completed before this one.

e2e.description: The E2E test row of the epic. Every epic MUST have one; null only in exceptional cases, during intermediate drafts.

label.description: Label of the E2E row, e.g. 'E2E test of the Epic Name flow'.

days.description: Working days for the E2E test.

assumptions.description: Assumptions the estimator made to produce the estimate (e.g. 'Basic validation = required fields plus a maximum amount check, no cross-field logic'). They must be made explicit: they are the first thing to revisit if the estimate turns out to be wrong. Sheet 'Assumptions and Considerations' in the Excel export.

considerations.description: General considerations about the project or the estimate (risk, dependencies, notes on the team, patterns applied). Sheet 'Assumptions and Considerations' in the Excel export, below the assumptions.

open_questions.description: Open questions for the client or user that, once answered, could change the estimate (e.g. 'Can an attachment have attachments of its own?'). Sheet 'Questions for the Users' in the Excel export.

area.description: Area or epic the question refers to (e.g. 'Data model', 'Notifications').

estimated_impact.description: Estimated impact on the estimate if the answer changes the current assumptions (e.g. '+1 day if yes'). Free-form string, qualitative or quantitative.
